name: AI in Federal Government Procurement
description: The General Services Administration (GSA) implemented artificial intelligence technology to streamline federal government procurement processes, which involve spending around $100 billion a year on goods and services. The initiative includes providing a guide to federal workers on best practices for incorporating AI into public services.
novelty: The innovation lies in using AI to enhance data insights and streamline procurement processes in the federal government, leveraging large language models and governmental data.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal government procurement officers, federal agencies, and ultimately taxpayers who benefit from more efficient government spending.

name: Autonomous Public Bus Services Pilot
description: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) is launching a pilot program to test autonomous public bus services. The pilot will start with smaller buses with at least 16 seats on Services 400 and 191. The goal is to assess the technical feasibility and operational aspects of autonomous vehicle technology for public bus services. The pilot will run for an initial period of three years starting mid-2026.
novelty: This project is innovative as it involves the integration of autonomous vehicle technology into public transportation, specifically for bus services, which includes real-time monitoring and a phased approach to ensure safety and reliability.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Land Transport Authority (LTA), SBS Transit Ltd (SBST), the selected tenderer, bus captains as safety operators, customer service officers, and the commuting public in Singapore.

name: BharatGen
description: BharatGen is the world's first government-funded multimodal large language model (LLM) initiative. It aims to develop a language model tailored to India's linguistic and cultural diversity, enhancing India's influence in AI research and application.
novelty: BharatGen is innovative as it is the first government-funded initiative to create a multimodal large language model specifically designed for a country's diverse linguistic and cultural landscape.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Ministry of Science and Technology, AI researchers, developers, and the diverse linguistic communities of India who will benefit from AI applications tailored to their cultural context.

name: Generative AI (Gen AI) Integration
description: The Trump administration's deregulation efforts aim to accelerate the development and integration of Generative AI (Gen AI) across various sectors, including public services and businesses. This initiative allows for the rapid deployment of AI-driven systems without extensive regulatory scrutiny, potentially increasing productivity and transforming service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in the removal of regulatory constraints, allowing for faster experimentation and integration of Gen AI, which can produce human-like text, images, and code at an unprecedented scale.
stakeholders: Stakeholders include businesses, public sector agencies, technology developers, and end-users such as citizens and consumers who benefit from enhanced services and productivity.

name: TraffEase
organisation: Loughborough University, Nottingham City Council, Transport for Greater Manchester
description: TraffEase is a research project exploring the application of generative AI innovations in transport, aiming to demonstrate scalable AI solutions for public services.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on generative AI applications in transport, showcasing scalable solutions and the art of the possible in AI-driven public service improvements.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include local authorities, policymakers, and the general public benefiting from improved transport services. Stakeholders engaged include Loughborough University researchers, Nottingham City Council, Transport for Greater Manchester, and national policymakers.

name: Claude
description: The UK government is exploring the use of Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude to improve interactions with public information and services, aiming to boost productivity in public services through AI.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves using an advanced AI chatbot to enhance accessibility and efficiency in public services, aligning with the UK's goal to become an AI superpower.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public and businesses in the UK, with stakeholders including the UK government and Anthropic.

name: Claude
description: The UK government is exploring the use of Anthropic's AI chatbot, Claude, to improve interactions with public information and services. This initiative aims to enhance productivity in public services through AI.
novelty: The project is innovative as it involves using a cutting-edge AI chatbot to streamline access to public information, potentially transforming how citizens interact with government services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the UK government, public service users, and Anthropic as the technology provider.

name: Advanced AI Technologies for Public Services
organisation: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) and Anthropic
description: The UK government and Anthropic have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to explore the use of advanced AI technologies to enhance public services for UK citizens. The collaboration aims to improve how citizens access government information and services online, while ensuring privacy and building trust.
novelty: The project focuses on the responsible deployment of frontier AI capabilities in the public sector, leveraging Anthropic's AI model, Claude, to enhance citizen experiences and improve government service responsiveness.
stakeholders: UK citizens, UK government, Anthropic, AI Security Institute, and potentially other government agencies and organizations involved in AI innovation and deployment.

name: Simply Readable
description: Simply Readable is a Claude-based tool hosted on Amazon Bedrock that reformats documents to be more accessible for people with disabilities by increasing font size, spacing, and adding images. It significantly reduces the cost of converting documents, allowing funds to be redirected to other social services.
novelty: The tool offers a cost-effective solution for making documents accessible, achieving a 749,900 percent return on investment by reducing conversion costs from around £600 to just 7-10 pence per document.
stakeholders: The primary beneficiaries are people with disabilities who require accessible documents. Swindon Borough Council and the UK's Local Government Association are stakeholders involved in implementing and promoting the tool.

name: AI in Tamil Nadu's E-Governance
description: Tamil Nadu is leveraging AI to enhance e-governance by implementing AI-driven monitoring systems across various departments to improve citizen service delivery. This includes digitalizing land, revenue, and registration-related transactions and exploring the use of OpenAI's models to speed up e-sevai services.
novelty: The innovation lies in integrating AI with existing e-governance systems to monitor application processes, introduce public reporting systems, and use intelligent systems for departmental performance assessment, potentially disrupting traditional bureaucratic processes.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include Tamil Nadu's government departments, citizens who use public services, and private sector entities involved in AI development. Beneficiaries are the general public who will experience improved service delivery.

name: DeepSeek
organisation: Meizhou government, Urban Operation Management Center in Wuxi, Futian district in Shenzhen, Second Affiliated Hospital of Fujian Medical University
description: DeepSeek is an AI technology integrated into public service systems to streamline tasks such as intelligent text-based chatbot responses, AI-assisted form filling, intelligent work order classification, and automated case routing. It improves response speed and resolution time for public inquiries and assists in administrative governance and clinical assistance.
novelty: DeepSeek introduces AI capabilities into public services, reducing call waiting times and improving document processing accuracy. It enhances productivity by providing rapid responses and relevant data pools, and supports analysis in various sectors, including government and healthcare.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include citizens using public service hotlines, government employees, community workers, and hospital staff. Stakeholders engaged include local government bodies, hospitals, and AI experts.

name: AI-powered chatbot "Reggie"
description: An AI-powered chatbot named "Reggie" facilitates real-time, two-way communication for Kent County residents, providing instant answers to inquiries by pulling data from multiple systems such as real estate property records and public meeting schedules.
novelty: Reggie not only answers questions but learns from interactions, improving its responses over time. It enhances citizen services by offering instant information and reducing wait times for county office inquiries.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are Kent County residents who need information about county services. Stakeholders include the Kent County IT department and local government officials.

name: Modernization of IT Procurement Processes
organisation: Pennsylvania Department of General Services’ Bureau of Procurement, Commonwealth Office of Digital Experience (CODE PA)
description: The innovation involves streamlining and standardizing Pennsylvania's IT procurement practices by removing unnecessary bureaucracy, increasing competition for state contracts, and protecting taxpayer dollars from project delays and cost overruns. Key changes include reducing the number of questions agencies must answer before purchasing IT by 75%, consolidating IT policies, and piloting generative AI for policy drafting.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its approach of breaking down IT projects into smaller, manageable scopes, using generative AI for policy drafting, and creating a new digital service called Invitation to Qualify (ITQ) for vendor pre-qualification.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state agencies in Pennsylvania, IT vendors, and taxpayers who benefit from more efficient and cost-effective procurement processes.

name: TrackInspect
organisation: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), Google Public Sector
description: TrackInspect is a pilot program that retrofits subway trains with sensors and uses cloud and AI capabilities to detect and analyze potential track defects. It aims to enhance the track inspection process, making the subway system safer and more reliable by detecting potential track issues before they escalate into operational problems.
novelty: The innovation integrates sensor hardware with advanced cloud and AI capabilities, including Generative AI for natural language processing, to proactively detect track defects. It is the first of its kind to use AI technology for subway maintenance, combining real-time sensor data with predictive insights to improve railway reliability.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include subway riders and track inspectors. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation are the MTA, Google Public Sector, and New York City Transit (NYCT) track inspectors.

name: GenAI-powered tools for public auditing
description: The UK’s Government Internal Audit Agency (GIAA) has developed GenAI-powered tools, including Risk Engine and Writing Engine, to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of audit management. These tools assist auditors in identifying risks related to business objectives and reduce manual effort in writing audit reports.
novelty: The innovation lies in the application of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) tools to improve audit management processes, enhancing risk identification and reducing manual reporting efforts.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include public auditors in the UK, the Government Internal Audit Agency (GIAA), and the broader public finance sector that benefits from improved audit processes.

name: Generative AI Chatbot for Benefits Communication
description: Xerox developed a generative AI chatbot specifically trained on the new paid family leave program to handle employee inquiries efficiently. This chatbot provides accurate and contextually relevant responses, improving employee access to information and satisfaction.
novelty: The chatbot uses generative AI to engage in fluid, conversational interactions, understanding nuances and adapting to different phrasing, unlike traditional rule-based chatbots.
stakeholders: The primary users are the county's public employees, with the Human Resources department benefiting from reduced inquiry volumes, allowing them to focus on complex tasks.

name: Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO)
organisation: Department of Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
description: The Regulatory Innovation Office (RIO) aims to cut red tape, remove barriers to innovation, and accelerate the time it takes to bring emerging technologies to market. It focuses on areas like engineering biology, space, AI in healthcare, and autonomous technology.
novelty: The RIO is innovative in its approach to shaping regulatory frameworks that empower new technologies, incorporating existing functions of the Regulatory Horizons Council and the Regulators’ Pioneer Fund to streamline innovation.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include technology innovators, businesses, and industries such as healthcare and transport. Stakeholders engaged include government departments like the Department for Transport, Department for Health and Social Care, and Department for Environment Food and Rural Affairs.

name: Generative AI in Procurement
organisation: Office of Information Technology Services, Idaho
description: The Office of Information Technology Services in Idaho is experimenting with generative AI to accelerate the procurement process for public transit systems. This approach aims to reduce the time it takes to complete procurement cycles, which can sometimes take years.
novelty: The use of generative AI in the procurement process is innovative as it seeks to significantly reduce the time required for procurement cycles, which is traditionally a lengthy and complex process.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Idaho Office of Information Technology Services, transit agencies, and potentially vendors involved in the procurement process. Beneficiaries would be the transit agencies and their passengers, who would experience improved services and technologies.

name: Agentic AI for Scholar Management
description: IMDA implemented low-code technologies, intelligent workflows, and AI-powered chatbots to automate its scholar management process, making it more efficient and user-friendly. This included developing over 50 workflows and integrating chatbot interactions, saving more than 1,000 man-hours over 12 months.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI, which acts as a 'digital twin' or 'nervous system', capable of understanding the context of problems and executing next steps autonomously, transforming internal operations into a cohesive ecosystem.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include IMDA staff and scholars, while stakeholders engaged include public sector agencies and Workato as the technology partner.

name: Combat Autonomous Maritime Platform (CAMP)
description: The Combat Autonomous Maritime Platform (CAMP) is a project by the Defense Innovation Unit to develop commercially available uncrewed underwater vehicles with exceptional range and payload capacity. These vehicles are intended to enhance the U.S. Navy's capabilities by providing scalable and cost-effective solutions for long-range, high-capacity payload deployment in contested environments.
novelty: CAMP represents a new class of uncrewed underwater vehicles that are significantly larger, have much longer range, and can carry larger payloads compared to previous prototypes like the LDUUV. They are designed to be pier launched and can operate in GPS-denied environments, offering enhanced communication capabilities across the air/water interface.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the U.S. Navy and other organizations within the Department of Defense. Beneficiaries are the U.S. military personnel who will have reduced risk exposure, and the vendors who will participate in the development and prototyping phases.

name: Agentic AI
organisation: JTC Corporation, Singapore's Ministry of Health (MOH), Digital and Intelligence Service (DIS), Workato
description: Agentic AI is being integrated into public sector systems to perform tasks autonomously, enhancing efficiency and quality of services. It is used in areas like building management and healthcare to streamline processes and improve decision-making.
novelty: Agentic AI can reason, plan, remember, and act without continuous human intervention, making it a transformative tool for enhancing public service efficiency.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include public sector employees, facility managers, healthcare professionals, and ultimately the general public benefiting from improved services. Stakeholders engaged include government agencies and technology providers.

name: Artificial Intelligence Playbook for the UK Government
description: The UK Government has published the Artificial Intelligence Playbook to integrate AI into public services, aiming to position the UK as a global AI leader. The playbook outlines initiatives like deploying large language models to improve citizen interactions and exploring AI applications in policymaking, supply-chain management, and scientific research.
novelty: The innovation lies in the UK's attempt to define a 'third way' on AI policy, balancing between the EU's overregulation and the US's underregulation, and its focus on integrating AI into public services to modernize government operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include UK citizens, who are the primary beneficiaries of improved public services, and the UK Government, which is responsible for implementing the AI initiatives. Private companies like Anthropic are also involved as partners in deploying AI technologies.

name: TrojAI
description: TrojAI is a program aimed at developing means to detect adversarial attacks on AI systems, particularly focusing on ensuring that large language models (LLMs) can be trained on classified data without leaking it. The program addresses challenges such as training skews, hallucinations, and prompt injections in AI models.
novelty: The program focuses on understanding and mitigating the risks associated with training large language models on classified data, ensuring they do not leak sensitive information even when manipulated through techniques like jailbreaking or prompt injections.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are intelligence agencies and the broader intelligence community, who are the users and beneficiaries of secure AI systems. Vendors like Microsoft and Palantir, who are working to integrate LLMs into classified networks, are also stakeholders.

name: PLLuM
description: PLLuM is Poland's first large language model tailored for public administration, designed to streamline processes and support government teams. It aims to make AI an integral tool for public service, providing a ready-made chatbot tool to ease administrative burdens, especially for smaller municipalities.
novelty: PLLuM is innovative as it is a country-specific large language model designed specifically for public administration, making it a practical tool for government services and supporting smaller municipalities with AI capabilities.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public servants, government teams, smaller municipalities, and potentially the private sector who can benefit from the model's applications.

name: Generative AI Technologies for City Services
description: The City of Anchorage, under the leadership of its first chief innovation officer, Brendan Babb, is experimenting with generative AI technologies to reduce friction in accessing city services. This initiative is part of a broader effort to cultivate a culture of innovation within the city's public services.
novelty: The project introduces generative AI technologies in the public sector to streamline access to city services, representing a novel approach in municipal governance.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the residents of Anchorage who will benefit from improved access to city services, as well as government employees who are being trained in new techniques.

name: GenAI Technologies in California State Government
description: The California State Government is implementing GenAI technologies across various departments to improve efficiency, enhance traffic management, increase road safety, and improve customer service. These technologies include AI solutions for traffic congestion, safety analysis, customer service enhancement, and productivity tools for state departments.
novelty: This initiative is pioneering in its comprehensive use of GenAI technologies across state government operations, including the first-in-the-nation digital democracy platform, and the first-of-its-kind AI collaboration with NVIDIA.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include California state departments such as Caltrans and CDTFA, technology partners like Microsoft, Accenture, Deloitte, Anthropic, and NVIDIA, as well as the general public, including vulnerable road users and taxpayers.

name: Generative AI Initiative in California State Government
description: The initiative integrates generative AI technologies across California's state government to improve traffic flow, road safety, and customer service. It involves using AI to analyze highway data to reduce congestion, streamline customer service systems, and enhance public safety.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of generative AI to enhance government efficiency and service delivery, leveraging California's unique procurement process to test and scale tech projects quickly.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include California residents, particularly those affected by traffic congestion and in need of improved public services. Stakeholders include the California Department of Transportation, Department of Tax and Fee Administration, and the California Government Operations Agency.

name: AI Employees
description: The innovation involves the creation of 'AI employees' to support human decision-making in government operations. This initiative aims to deploy thousands of AI employees across federal agencies to enhance government services and research.
novelty: The project introduces the concept of 'AI employees' as distinct from 'AI agents,' emphasizing the need for significant investment in AI infrastructure, training, and risk management to maintain and enhance performance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, government employees, and potentially the general public who will benefit from improved government services.

name: Prompt Engineering Training Pilot
description: A pilot program in Pennsylvania where 175 employees from 14 agencies used ChatGPT Enterprise to enhance their skills in prompt engineering, which is crucial for effectively utilizing generative AI. The program aimed to train non-technical staff to craft effective prompts for AI models, resulting in over 85% of participants reporting a positive experience.
novelty: First-of-its-kind pilot focusing on training public sector employees in prompt engineering to improve their interaction with generative AI.
stakeholders: Public sector employees in Pennsylvania, particularly non-technical staff from 14 different agencies, and state tech leaders.

name: KolokoloChat
organisation: Hawaiʻi State Judiciary, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa
description: KolokoloChat is an AI-powered chatbot that provides 24/7 access to court information and resources, including FAQs, online forms, and self-help resources for court users. It uses natural language processing to engage users in real-time conversations, guiding them to relevant information to make court interactions more efficient and accessible.
novelty: The innovation lies in its use of artificial intelligence and natural language processing to make complex legal information more accessible and easier to navigate, enhancing public access to court resources.
stakeholders: Users include court users and self-represented litigants. Beneficiaries are the people of Hawaiʻi who need access to legal information. Stakeholders involved include the Hawaiʻi State Judiciary, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, IBM, Google, and Research CyberInfrastructure.

name: K-AI Based Government
description: The K-AI Based Government initiative aims to transition South Korea's government from a Digital Platform Government to an AI Government by developing a generative AI (GenAI) common foundation. This shared AI infrastructure will enable public agencies to improve public services through AI technology, focusing on operational efficiency and public service delivery.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its emphasis on creating a generative AI common foundation that leverages public-private partnerships and private sector cloud and AI solutions. It also focuses on using South Korean large language models and AI platforms, operating on a usage-based subscription model.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include South Korean government departments, public officers, public transport companies, young farmers in rural communities, and private cloud service providers. Beneficiaries are the general public who will experience improved public services.

name: AI Genomics Schema Harmonizer
organisation: Digital Transformation Hub (DxHub) at California Polytechnic State University, Wisconsin State Laboratory of Hygiene (WSLH), Virginia Department of General Services Division of Consolidated Laboratory Services (DCLS)
description: AI Genomics Schema Harmonizer is a generative AI-powered application that automates the alignment of diverse laboratory terminologies with standardized formats for genomic data submission to public repositories, significantly reducing the time and errors associated with manual data preparation.
novelty: The innovation uses generative AI and natural language processing to automatically standardize genomic data, eliminating the need for manual data transformations and increasing accuracy by reducing typographical errors.
stakeholders: Public health laboratories, genomic researchers, public health partners, and the broader scientific community are the primary users and beneficiaries of this innovation.

name: EVA
organisation: Union County Department of Human Services, TipCo Automated Systems
description: EVA is an AI assistant designed to support the Union County Department of Human Services Action Line by handling high volumes of client inquiries across various services, such as SNAP, TANF, Medicaid, and more. It aims to improve accessibility, reduce wait times, and free up staff for complex casework.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to enhance the efficiency and accessibility of human services, allowing for timely information access and improved resource allocation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include Union County residents who are the users and beneficiaries of the services, the Union County Department of Human Services, and TipCo Automated Systems.

name: Digital Platform Government (DPG)
description: The Digital Platform Government (DPG) initiative in South Korea aims to provide integrated, personalised, and proactive services from the citizen's perspective by leveraging high-quality, interoperable open data and AI technologies. The initiative focuses on integrating data across various government agencies and strengthening public-private cooperation to deliver platform-based governance.
novelty: The innovation shifts from traditional e-government to a digital platform government model, emphasizing integration of data and public-private cooperation to provide personalized services. It leverages a common generative AI foundation and cloud-native technologies to enhance flexibility and service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include South Korean citizens who receive improved public services. Stakeholders engaged include central ministries, local governments, public institutions, and private sector companies providing AI and cloud technologies.

name: Minute
description: 'Minute' is an AI tool designed to automate note-taking and streamline administrative tasks in local councils, helping to improve efficiency in services such as planning and social care. It is part of the 'Humphrey' suite of AI tools aimed at enhancing public service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in its use of generative AI to automate meeting note-taking, reducing administrative burdens and allowing officials to focus on more critical tasks. It is the first time AI has been used in a meeting chaired by a UK Prime Minister.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are local council officials and staff, including those involved in planning and social care. The broader beneficiaries are local residents who receive improved services.

name: Generative AI for Legislative Cost Analysis
description: The California Department of Finance is using generative AI tools to analyze the cost and budget impact of legislative bills. This innovation aims to reduce the manual workload of drafting bill summaries and analyses, and improve the efficiency and accuracy of financial analysis for policymakers.
novelty: The project leverages generative AI to automate and enhance the analysis of legislative bills, integrating AI into government workflows to modernize public service delivery.
stakeholders: State employees, California policymakers, and the general public who benefit from more efficient and transparent government services.

name: Generative AI for Public Services in California
description: The California Department of Technology, under the leadership of State Chief Technology Innovation Officer Vera Zakem, is implementing generative AI tools to improve public services such as traffic management, wildfire response, and digital service delivery. The initiative aims to enhance government efficiency and service delivery to Californians.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating generative AI across various public sectors, focusing on transparency, accountability, and responsible use. It includes the first-in-the-nation deployment of GenAI technology for government efficiency and digital service delivery.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include California residents, government agencies, technology developers, and industry partners. Beneficiaries are the citizens of California who will experience improved public services.

name: Uncrewed Triple Challenge (UTC)
organisation: Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC), Michigan Department of Transportation (MDOT), Department of Military and Veterans Affairs (DMVA), Michigan National Guard (MING)
description: The Uncrewed Triple Challenge (UTC) is a state-sponsored competition in Michigan that tests autonomous drones across air, land, and water. It aims to push the boundaries of autonomous drone capabilities and establish Michigan as a leader in uncrewed systems and advanced mobility.
novelty: The UTC is the first-of-its-kind competition that challenges drones to complete a three-part course autonomously, covering maritime, airborne, and ground routes without human intervention.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector entities, defense industry, tech entrepreneurs, and participants from seven U.S. states and two countries. Beneficiaries include the defense and manufacturing sectors, as well as potential future users of autonomous systems for tasks like delivering medicine and equipment.

name: Generative AI-powered Virtual Assistant
organisation: North Carolina Department of Commerce’s Division of Employment Security (NCDES)
description: An intelligent virtual assistant powered by generative AI to help individuals navigate the unemployment insurance claim application process, providing consistent answers 24/7 and reducing call volumes.
novelty: One of the first generative AI-powered, public-facing solutions in North Carolina, integrating AI to enhance citizen services while improving operational efficiency.
stakeholders: Users are individuals filing unemployment claims; beneficiaries include North Carolinians seeking unemployment benefits; stakeholders include NCDES, AWS, and Ernst & Young.

name: AI Agents for HR and Financial Operations
description: AI agents are used to streamline HR processes by automating job description creation, candidate shortlisting, and policy compliance. This allows HR managers to focus on strategic support rather than manual processes. The AI also aids in policy analysis and contract intelligence, improving efficiency and decision-making in government operations.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI agents to automate and enhance HR and financial operations, reducing manual effort and increasing efficiency in government agencies. It also includes AI-driven insights for skills-based talent strategies and policy compliance.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are HR managers, government agencies, and public sector employees. The stakeholders engaged include government policymakers and public sector organisations.

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
description: A trial involving over 20,000 civil servants using Microsoft 365 Copilot, a generative AI tool, to assist with tasks such as drafting documents, summarising emails, and updating records. The trial demonstrated significant time savings, with users saving an average of 26 minutes per day, equating to nearly two weeks per year.
novelty: The innovation lies in the large-scale application of AI to routine government tasks, demonstrating substantial productivity gains and the potential to transform public service delivery by reducing administrative burdens.
stakeholders: The primary users are civil servants across various departments, including policy officials and Work Coaches. Beneficiaries include job seekers and the general public who receive more efficient public services.

name: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) in Australian Public Sector
description: The Australian public sector is exploring the use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) to improve productivity and efficiency in policy work. The focus is on using GenAI for administrative tasks that do not involve sensitive data, with the aim of augmenting rather than replacing human workers.
novelty: The innovation lies in the cautious and experimental approach to integrating GenAI in public sector policy work, with clear outcomes and evaluation processes to ensure value for money and risk mitigation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include senior public servants, government agencies, and the Australian public, who are the beneficiaries of improved public services.

name: AI and Biometrics Strategy
description: The AI and biometrics strategy aims to ensure organisations develop and deploy AI and biometric technologies lawfully, supporting innovation and growth while protecting public privacy. It includes reviewing automated decision-making systems, auditing facial recognition technology use, setting expectations for AI model training, and developing a statutory code of practice.
novelty: The strategy focuses on scrutinising emerging AI risks and trends, such as agentic AI, and aims to balance innovation with privacy protection by setting clear expectations and developing a statutory code of practice.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include the general public, organisations using AI and biometric technologies, and government bodies like the Department for Work and Pensions. Stakeholders engaged include Parliamentarians, industry representatives, and civil society.

name: Self-driving vehicle pilots
description: The UK government is fast-tracking pilots for self-driving taxi- and bus-like services, allowing them to operate without a safety driver from 2026. These services will be available for public booking via an app, aiming to improve road safety, create jobs, and boost the economy.
novelty: The innovation involves the introduction of self-driving vehicles without safety drivers for public use, supported by robust legislation and safety measures, positioning the UK as a leader in autonomous vehicle technology.
stakeholders: Users include the general public who will book the services via an app. Beneficiaries include local communities, particularly in rural areas, and those unable to drive. Stakeholders engaged include the UK government, technology companies like Wayve and Oxa, and partners such as Uber and Nissan.

name: AI Governance and Integration in Public Services
description: Several states in the U.S. have established public-private AI task forces to assess risks and opportunities and make recommendations on leveraging AI for public service delivery. North Carolina, in particular, has partnered with OpenAI to use ChatGPT for analyzing publicly available data to improve government service efficiency.
novelty: The innovation lies in the collaboration between state governments and the private sector to establish AI governance frameworks and integrate AI technologies like ChatGPT into public services, ensuring ethical and efficient use.
stakeholders: State governments, private sector companies, public service users, citizens benefiting from improved government services.

name: FedRAMP High and DOD Impact Levels 4 and 5 Authorization for Claude and Llama AI Models
description: Amazon Web Services has received federal authorizations allowing Anthropic’s Claude and Meta’s Llama AI models to be used within high-sensitivity government computing environments. This includes achieving FedRAMP 'High' authorization and the Defense Department’s Impact Levels 4 and 5, enabling the use of these AI models in AWS GovCloud for sensitive civilian and military information.
novelty: This is the first time a cloud provider has received such a high level of authorization for Claude and Llama AI models, allowing them to be used in high-security government environments.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, defense agencies, and public sector entities that require secure and compliant access to advanced AI tools for mission-critical applications.

name: HealthHub AI
description: HealthHub AI is an innovation that integrates AI agents into the national healthcare app HealthHub, allowing users to book appointments and access health information through a conversational assistant. It aims to make healthcare services more accessible and personalized by customizing health content and recommendations based on users' life stages and healthcare needs.
novelty: The innovation is novel due to its use of AI agents to execute tasks within a healthcare app, enhancing user experience by simplifying appointment bookings and providing multilingual support. It also incorporates local-language large language models to expand language accessibility.
stakeholders: Users of the HealthHub app, public healthcare institutions, private sector partners, and developers using healthcare APIs are the primary stakeholders. Beneficiaries include patients and healthcare providers who will experience improved service accessibility and efficiency.

name: HKPilot
organisation: Digital Policy Office (DPO), Civil Service College (CSC), Hong Kong Generative AI Research and Development Center (HKGAI)
description: HKPilot is a generative AI-based document processing assistant designed to support civil servants with tasks such as document drafting, translation, and summarisation. It aims to reduce administrative workload, enhance efficiency, and free up time for strategic work.
novelty: HKPilot is innovative as it is a locally developed AI application tailored specifically for government use in Hong Kong, taking into account the city's linguistic, cultural, and policy contexts.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include civil servants in various government bureaux and departments, as well as the general public who will benefit from more efficient public services.

name: AI.gov
description: AI.gov is a federal website designed to accelerate government innovation with AI. It includes tools for analyzing agency-wide AI implementation, an AI chatbot, and an API to connect federal systems with private AI models. The initiative aims to integrate AI into federal agencies and promote public-private AI partnerships.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to integrating AI into federal agencies, promoting public-private partnerships, and providing tools like a groundbreaking analysis console, AI chatbot, and API for system integration.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, private sector partners, and ultimately, the American public who will benefit from improved government services through AI integration.

name: GenAI Applications in Public Sector
description: Public sector organisations are increasingly adopting Generative AI (GenAI) applications to improve constituent and employee support, experience solutions, and content generation. The focus is on leveraging AI to enhance productivity, automation, and efficiency while addressing security and privacy concerns.
novelty: The innovation lies in the widespread adoption of GenAI applications across the public sector, transforming traditional processes and prioritizing data security and privacy in AI implementations.
stakeholders: Public sector leaders, employees, constituents, IT decision-makers, and organisations leveraging GenAI applications.

name: FUZE Innovation Engine
description: FUZE is an innovation engine designed to accelerate the development and deployment of emerging military technologies by funding prototypes quickly and scaling successful technologies in months instead of years. It aims to bridge the gap between early prototyping and fully fielded capabilities, focusing on technologies like artificial intelligence and autonomous systems.
novelty: FUZE combines venture capital-style agility with rapid operational feedback, allowing for a 'fail fast' approach that departs from traditional, risk-averse military acquisition processes. It emphasizes rapid prototyping and real-world testing to ensure technologies meet operational needs.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are the United States Army, including soldiers who benefit from faster deployment of new technologies, and technology companies that participate in the innovation process. Forward-deployed acquisition officers in Europe, Africa, and the Pacific also play a role in providing operational feedback.

name: AI-native public infrastructure
organisation: Various cities including Singapore, Barcelona, Copenhagen, Amsterdam, Los Angeles, New York City, Dubai, Pittsburgh, Hangzhou, Seoul
description: AI-native public infrastructure transforms city management by integrating AI, sensing, and data infrastructure to manage complex systems like transport, energy, and water as continuously operating systems. This approach allows for real-time decision-making and autonomous operations, improving efficiency and reducing disruptions.
novelty: The innovation lies in shifting from traditional smart-city models to AI-native models where technology not only observes but also decides and acts autonomously within policy constraints. This enables cities to operate like distributed computing systems with real-time data processing and autonomous control.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include city residents and municipal service providers. Stakeholders engaged include city governments, technology providers, and infrastructure management entities.

name: AI Integration in Raleigh's City Services
description: Raleigh, N.C. has expanded its use of AI across city services, including internal chatbots for city employees, traffic analysis systems, and experimental AI in municipal services. The city is testing AI to automate IT service requests and analyzing traffic patterns using visual language models.
novelty: The innovation lies in the integration of AI to not only respond to queries but to perform tasks autonomously, such as managing IT service requests and analyzing traffic patterns with generative AI capabilities.
stakeholders: City employees, residents of Raleigh, city planners, IT department, Solid Waste Services department, and regional cybersecurity collaborators.

name: VA Digital Modernization and AI Initiatives
description: The VA technology team modernized VA.gov into a veteran-focused digital platform, launched a flagship mobile application, expanded access to generative AI tools, deployed clinical decision support tools, and used AI-powered document processing to eliminate a backlog.
novelty: The integration of AI into VA systems, including generative AI tools for employees, clinical decision support tools, and AI-powered document processing, represents a significant technological shift in public service delivery.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include veterans, VA employees, clinicians, and developers. Stakeholders engaged include the Department of Veterans Affairs and its technology team.

name: USAi
organisation: General Services Administration (GSA), Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI)
description: USAi is a secure generative AI platform and centralized procurement toolbox designed to enable federal agencies to confidently adopt AI at scale. It expands the federal government’s AI infrastructure by providing shared services for agencies to experiment with and adopt AI systems.
novelty: The innovation lies in the creation of a centralized platform that not only facilitates the adoption of AI across federal agencies but also incorporates robust methodologies for evaluating AI performance, security, and functionality within real-world workflows.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are federal agencies that will use the USAi platform for AI adoption. Beneficiaries include the American public, as the platform aims to enhance government services through AI. CAISI and GSA are key partners in this initiative.

name: Xoli
organisation: Government of Mexico City, Digital Agency for Public Innovation, Ministries of Tourism and Culture
description: Xoli is a chatbot launched by the Government of Mexico City to provide information on services, tourism, and cultural offerings via WhatsApp in both English and Spanish. It aims to support the influx of visitors during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and will remain active post-event to promote economic activities and facilitate access to public services.
novelty: Xoli is innovative as it integrates cultural, tourism, recreation, and entertainment information into a single, accessible platform via WhatsApp, offering real-time, multilingual support to users.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include tourists, residents of Mexico City, and businesses involved in tourism and cultural activities. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the Government of Mexico City, the Digital Agency for Public Innovation, and the local Ministries of Tourism and Culture.

name: Agent Kyle
description: Agent Kyle is an AI agent launched by the city of Kyle, Texas, to assist with 311 customer service calls, aiming to improve service delivery by handling routine inquiries and tasks.
novelty: Agent Kyle represents the integration of agentic AI in public service, showcasing how AI can transform service delivery by automating routine tasks and allowing human workers to focus on more meaningful work.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include residents of Kyle, Texas, who use the 311 service, and city employees who can focus on more complex tasks. Stakeholders include city government officials and technology providers.

name: Low-cost Uncrewed Combat Attack System (LUCAS)
organisation: Indiana National Guard, Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering
description: LUCAS is an affordable, autonomous combat attack system that was rapidly advanced from public demonstration to operational employment in just seven months, showcasing a new model for defense innovation.
novelty: The innovation lies in the rapid transition from concept to operational deployment, achieved through the T-REX framework and the 'speed of relevance' methodology, which accelerates traditional defense acquisition timelines.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Indiana National Guard, the Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering, operators, engineers, acquisition professionals, and the Joint Force as beneficiaries.

name: AI Executive Order N‑5‑26
organisation: California Department of Technology (CDT), Department of General Services (DGS), California Government Operations Agency
description: The Executive Order N‑5‑26 issued by California Governor Gavin Newsom aims to leverage generative AI for the benefit of Californians while ensuring transparency, privacy, and civil liberties protection. It mandates new trust and safety obligations for AI companies contracting with California agencies and updates procurement processes to include transparency and privacy requirements.
novelty: The project is innovative as it establishes distinct state procurement standards for AI that differ from federal requirements, focusing on transparency, privacy, and civil liberties protection in AI deployment.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include California government agencies and the general public. Stakeholders engaged include the California Department of Technology, Department of General Services, California Government Operations Agency, and other state departments.

name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server with Agentic AI
organisation: City of Boston, Bloomberg Philanthropies, Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) at MIT
description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server is a software layer that allows AI models to securely interact with Boston's open data portal. It includes a set of open-source 'skills' or workflow templates that enable complex analytical workflows to produce formatted tables and plain language answers to various questions, aiming to expand transparency, improve policymaking, and broaden participation in data-driven decision-making.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of agentic AI, which involves AI 'agents' that follow structured workflows to analyze data and provide insights, mimicking the processes of skilled data and policy analysts. This allows for more secure, accurate, and accessible data analysis.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include Boston city employees, residents, and potentially other cities that adopt the technology. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the City of Boston, Bloomberg Philanthropies, and J-PAL at MIT.

name: Executive Order N-5-26
description: Executive Order N-5-26 directs state agencies in California to develop new standards for AI companies seeking state contracts and to expand the responsible use of generative AI in government operations. It includes new certification requirements for state contractors, independent review of federal supply chain designations, reforms to contractor responsibility provisions, and guidance on watermarking AI-generated content.
novelty: The Order adds a procurement-focused layer to AI governance, emphasizing public procurement as a tool to shape market behavior and encourage responsible innovation. It also introduces new certification requirements and independent review mechanisms for AI-related state contracts.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state agencies in California, AI companies seeking to contract with the state, and the general public who will benefit from improved government transparency and accountability through the use of GenAI.

name: Gemini for Government
description: Gemini for Government is an AI platform that transforms public sector workflows by enabling the use of AI agents to streamline processes, enhance productivity, and deliver personalized services. It is used by agencies like the CDAO, DOT, FDA, and DOE to advance scientific discovery and improve constituent services.
novelty: The innovation lies in its ability to move beyond AI exploration to real-world applications at scale, empowering public sector employees to create and run AI agents in a secure environment.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector organizations such as the CDAO, DOT, FDA, and DOE, as well as their employees and the constituents they serve.

name: Gemini for Government
description: Gemini for Government is an advanced AI platform designed to empower public sector organizations by moving beyond AI exploration to real-world applications at scale. It aims to streamline business processes and administrative tasks, enhance productivity, and drive mission impact across various government agencies.
novelty: The innovation lies in its integrated tech stack that offers velocity, precision, and cost efficiency, along with a secure environment for deploying AI agents at scale, making it a powerful tool for public sector transformation.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include public sector agencies such as the U.S. Department of Transportation, the FDA, and the Department of Energy, as well as state and local governments like the State of Iowa and the City of Los Angeles. It involves 3 million civilian and military personnel.

name: ChatGPT Enterprise Pilot and Expansion
description: The Shapiro Administration expanded the use of generative AI tools to over 3,000 state employees across 35 agencies in Pennsylvania. The initiative aims to deliver better, faster services to Pennsylvanians by using AI for tasks like drafting communications, summarizing materials, and conducting research. The pilot demonstrated significant time savings and efficiency improvements.
novelty: First-in-the-nation pilot of ChatGPT Enterprise with Commonwealth employees, focusing on safe, human-centered AI expansion with strong governance and thoughtful implementation.
stakeholders: State employees, Commonwealth agencies, Pennsylvanians, Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Local 668, InnovateUS, Carnegie Mellon University, University of Pennsylvania.

name: AI Governance in China
organisation: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC), National Technical Committee on Cybersecurity Standardization (TC260)
description: In 2025, China's AI governance focused on balancing regulation and development, with efforts to refine generative AI content labelling, safety standards, and application norms. The governance framework included systematic policy and standards, scenario-based regulation, and coordinated enforcement to advance AI from high-speed to high-quality development.
novelty: The innovation lies in the comprehensive approach to AI governance, which includes a full-chain regulatory system, scenario-based regulation, and the integration of AI technology with key sectors while maintaining compliance and safety.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include regulatory authorities like CAC and TC260, AI service providers, distribution platforms, and industries integrating AI technology such as manufacturing, meteorology, and government affairs.

name: Diia.AI
organisation: Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine
description: Diia.AI is the world's first national AI agent designed for government services, aimed at improving the efficiency and accessibility of public services in Ukraine.
novelty: Diia.AI is innovative as it represents the first instance of a national AI agent being used for government services, setting a precedent for AI integration in public administration.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries of Diia.AI include Ukrainian citizens who interact with government services, as well as the Ministry of Digital Transformation of Ukraine, which is responsible for its implementation.

name: Mission Forward
description: Mission Forward is a philosophy that shifts the focus from AI that merely generates answers to systems that deliver mission outcomes. It emphasizes the integration of AI into operational layers to solve real-world problems, such as reducing backlogs and optimizing workforce efficiency.
novelty: The innovation lies in embedding AI into core systems to enable autonomous actions and deliver mission outcomes, rather than just generating insights. It focuses on operational integration and intelligent execution platforms.
stakeholders: Federal agencies, government IT leaders, public sector organizations, and companies like Blue Diamond and Maersk that benefit from AI-enabled systems.

name: International Civil Service Conference (ICSC) 2026
organisation: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (HCSF)
description: The International Civil Service Conference (ICSC) 2026 is a platform aimed at driving reforms and strengthening collaboration across public sector institutions. It focuses on deepening institutional resilience and promoting transformative governance across Africa and beyond. The conference will feature exhibition booths showcasing reforms and innovations, plenary sessions, strategic discussion stages, and executive roundtables.
novelty: The conference promotes a transition to a fully paperless civil service and highlights the deployment of the Enterprise Content Management System (ECMS) and the Federal Government’s AI-powered assistant, Service-Wise GPT, as key innovations enhancing efficiency and service delivery.
stakeholders: Heads of Civil Service, policymakers, development partners, academics, private sector leaders, and participants from Federal Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs).

name: Ceitec-GPT Semiconductor Partnership
description: A partnership between Brazilian state-owned chipmaker Ceitec and Chinese multinational GPT to produce semiconductors in Brazil, focusing on strategic sectors like energy, electric mobility, and the automotive industry. The initiative aims to enhance Brazil's semiconductor manufacturing capacity and technological sovereignty.
novelty: The partnership involves technology transfer and aims to integrate Brazil into global semiconductor supply chains, focusing on power semiconductors for energy transition applications.
stakeholders: Users and beneficiaries include sectors such as energy, electric mobility, and automotive industries. Stakeholders engaged include Ceitec, GPT, the Brazilian Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation, and the Ministry of Management and Innovation in Public Services.

name: National AI Partnership with Google and OpenAI
organisation: Google, OpenAI, Ministry of Digital Development and Information
description: Singapore has established a National AI Partnership with Google and a memorandum of understanding with OpenAI to enhance its AI ecosystem. The partnership aims to accelerate AI deployment across public services, healthcare, education, and enterprise, and includes setting up an AI lab in Singapore.
novelty: The partnership is innovative due to its comprehensive approach to strengthening Singapore's AI ecosystem, including significant financial commitments and a focus on solving societal challenges, building an AI-ready workforce, and creating a secure AI ecosystem.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Singapore government, Google, OpenAI, and the broader AI community in Singapore, including public service sectors, healthcare, education, and enterprises that will benefit from enhanced AI capabilities.

name: Generative AI in Government
description: The innovation involves the use of generative AI to transform the economics of drafting, summarizing, coding assistance, document review, and other language-heavy tasks within federal agencies. It aims to reduce costs and improve efficiency in routine cognitive work, allowing public managers to focus more on judgment-intensive tasks.
novelty: The innovation is novel because it leverages generative AI to significantly lower the cost of routine knowledge work, offering a 25-to-1 cost advantage over traditional methods. It emphasizes governance as a productivity strategy, ensuring AI is used effectively and safely in public services.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include federal agencies, government professionals, public managers, analysts, attorneys, acquisition staff, program managers, and early-career employees who are affected by the shift in work dynamics due to AI.

name: OpenText Aviator Studio
description: OpenText Aviator Studio allows government agencies to create AI agents to automate tasks specific to public sector use cases and environments. These AI agents can operate across different platforms like SAP, Microsoft, and OpenText to accomplish complex processes.
novelty: The innovation enables the creation of AI agents that can automate complex tasks across multiple platforms, enhancing operational efficiency and integration in public sector environments.
stakeholders: Government agencies, public sector employees, and citizens who benefit from more efficient and automated public services.

name: California's Statewide Generative AI Strategy
description: California implemented its first statewide generative AI strategy, which includes guidelines for evaluating AI tools, procurement practices, and risk management frameworks. The strategy has influenced other states' approaches to AI adoption and led to pilot projects in customer service, health care, traffic management, public safety, and language accessibility.
novelty: This was California's first executive order governing generative AI use in state government, setting a precedent for other states and focusing on practical AI applications rather than just tool development.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include state government agencies, technology companies involved in pilot projects, and the general public benefiting from improved public services.

name: Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (CAIO) Role
description: The Illinois Department of Innovation & Technology appointed its first Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (CAIO) to lead the state's enterprise AI strategy and support the responsible adoption of AI across government services. The role involves guiding state AI strategy, governance, and ethical deployment, and scaling up AI projects like the chatbot tool.
novelty: The creation of a dedicated Chief AI Officer role to oversee AI strategy, governance, and ethical deployment in state government is innovative, as it formalizes AI leadership and integrates AI governance into state operations.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Illinois state government, its departments, and the 55,000 users of the chatbot tool. Beneficiaries are the residents of Illinois who will experience improved government services through AI.

name: Mythos
description: Mythos is a frontier AI model developed by Anthropic, capable of identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities in software systems. It has been used to find thousands of vulnerabilities in financial institutions, allowing them to shore up defenses before potential exploitation by adversaries.
novelty: Mythos represents a significant advancement in AI capabilities, able to autonomously identify and exploit vulnerabilities at a scale and speed previously unattainable by human researchers.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include financial institutions, critical infrastructure operators, and government agencies that benefit from the model's ability to identify vulnerabilities. Additionally, AI developers and cybersecurity professionals are engaged in ensuring the model's safe deployment.

name: Next-generation Security Operations Center (SOC)
description: GDIT has expanded its partnership with Splunk to deliver advanced cybersecurity solutions for the U.S. government. The collaboration focuses on building a next-generation Security Operations Center (SOC) using AI to enhance threat detection and response, integrate cybersecurity data, and develop zero trust capabilities.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of agentic AI to detect, isolate, and remediate threats rapidly, and its integration of AI to correlate data from multiple sources for improved situational awareness.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders are U.S. federal agencies, including the U.S. Air Force and the Virginia Information Technologies Agency (VITA), which benefit from enhanced cybersecurity solutions. The users include government employees and residents served by these agencies.

name: Explainable AI Approach
description: NEC promotes an explainable AI approach, known as white-box AI, where the reasoning behind AI outputs can be communicated to leaders, ensuring accountability even with fragmented data. This approach includes functions like Quality Checker, LLM Explainer, and Fact Checker to support explainability, traceability, and transparency in the public sector.
novelty: The innovation lies in the development of explainable AI functions that enhance transparency and accountability in AI systems, addressing issues like LLM hallucination and ensuring that AI outputs can be justified and understood by human decision-makers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public sector agencies in Southeast Asia, NEC as the technology provider, and citizens who benefit from improved public services and accountability.

name: Agentic AI (AAI) Systems in Government Services
description: Agentic AI (AAI) systems are being explored to transform public services by enabling AI to plan, coordinate, and execute complex tasks autonomously. The UAE aims to transition 50% of its government sectors to AAI, promising reduced administrative burdens, improved accessibility, and seamless service delivery.
novelty: The innovation lies in moving beyond simple automation to autonomous AI systems that can coordinate actions across multiple government departments, offering a new phase of public-sector transformation.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government agencies, citizens, businesses, policymakers, and public service employees who will interact with or benefit from the AAI systems.

name: Poppy
description: Poppy is a generative AI assistant launched by the California Department of Technology to help public servants safely experiment with AI and build tools tailored to their operational needs. It operates on a secure, state-government network, pulling information only from official CA.gov websites to ensure data security.
novelty: Poppy is innovative as it provides a secure platform for public servants to experiment with AI, ensuring data security by only using information from official state websites. It moves from pilot to production status, expanding its availability across the state.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include public servants in California who will use Poppy to enhance their operational needs, and the residents of California who will benefit from improved government services.

name: Bangsamoro ICT Research and Innovation Conference
organisation: Bangsamoro Information and Communications Technology Office (BICTO)
description: The Bangsamoro ICT Research and Innovation Conference is an event organized by BICTO to showcase research studies aimed at addressing regional challenges, informing data-driven policymaking, and advancing technological innovation in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). The conference includes presentations, poster exhibits, and prototype displays to promote innovation and the adoption of emerging technologies.
novelty: The conference provides a unique platform for collaboration among government, academia, industry, and development partners, focusing on evidence-based policies and creative solutions to improve public services and create new opportunities in the region.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include government officials, academic researchers, industry professionals, and development partners, with beneficiaries being the residents of the Bangsamoro region who will benefit from improved public services and technological advancements.

name: Housing Helper
description: A generative AI-powered housing advice chatbot designed to help prevent homelessness by providing timely and personalised advice to individuals at risk.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of a generative AI chatbot specifically aimed at preventing homelessness, coupled with a rigorous evaluation through a randomised trial to assess its effectiveness in the public sector.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are individuals at risk of homelessness. Stakeholders include Southwark Council, policymakers, researchers, and evaluators involved in the trial and implementation.

name: Generative AI for Local Storm Reports (LSRs)
organisation: National Weather Service (NWS), Amazon Web Services (AWS)
description: The National Weather Service, in collaboration with AWS, developed a proof of concept using generative AI to automate the extraction and verification of weather and geolocation information from text and images for Local Storm Reports (LSRs). This innovation aims to streamline the creation of LSRs, increasing their consistency, coverage, and number while maintaining scientific accuracy.
novelty: The project uses a serverless AWS architecture and a human-in-the-loop AI system to automate and enhance the creation of Local Storm Reports, integrating generative AI and domain-specific verification agents to simulate expert meteorological review processes.
stakeholders: The primary users and beneficiaries are NWS forecasters and emergency management partners. The stakeholders include AWS, the Generative AI Innovation Center, and the broader meteorological community.

name: Claude AI Assistant
description: California has negotiated a contract with Anthropic to provide state and local agencies access to Claude, an AI assistant, at a 50% discounted rate. The aim is to streamline administrative work, analyze information, draft documents, and improve constituent services, while ensuring accuracy, transparency, and privacy.
novelty: The innovation lies in the enterprise agreement approach, allowing multiple agencies to share best practices and establish governance standards, rather than isolated AI pilots. It also emphasizes responsible and transparent use of AI to augment, not replace, human workers.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include California state and local government agencies, public employees, and ultimately the residents of California who benefit from improved public services.

name: Sovereign AI
description: SERPRO's Sovereign AI initiative aims to build a national AI platform using large language models trained in Portuguese, operated on Brazilian infrastructure, to modernize public-sector services, automate processes, improve decision-making, and reduce reliance on foreign technology.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its focus on creating a secure, locally governed AI platform tailored to Brazil's needs, emphasizing multilingual capabilities and public-grade performance.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Brazilian federal government, public-sector organizations, and potentially other companies in Brazil. Beneficiaries are the Brazilian public sector and citizens who will experience improved public services.

name: Xoli
organisation: Mexico City’s Agencia de Innovacion Publica, University of California San Diego Halıcıoğlu School of Data Science and Computing
description: Xoli is an AI-powered tourism assistant designed to help tourists navigate Mexico City, discover attractions, access local information, and receive real-time assistance during the FIFA World Cup. The system was tested and scaled to handle large numbers of simultaneous users, ensuring efficient performance during the event.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of advanced computing resources and expertise in deploying large language models to scale an AI assistant for real-time, large-scale public service applications during a major international event.
stakeholders: The users and beneficiaries include tourists visiting Mexico City for the FIFA World Cup. Stakeholders engaged in the innovation include the developers from Mexico City’s Agencia de Innovacion Publica and research scientists from the University of California San Diego.

name: Ultimate Urban Circulator
description: The Ultimate Urban Circulator is the nation's first deployment of an autonomous vehicle service in public transit, aimed at enhancing mobility solutions in Jacksonville.
novelty: It is innovative as it represents the first deployment of autonomous vehicles in public transit in the United States.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Jacksonville Transportation Authority, residents of Jacksonville and surrounding counties, and users of public transit in the region.

name: Improving Geneva Public Services via AI Agents
organisation: State of Geneva, Department of Institutions and Digital Technology
description: The project involves the use of AI agents to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of public services in Geneva. The initiative aims to streamline processes and improve service delivery to the public.
novelty: The innovation lies in the application of AI technology to public services, which is expected to transform traditional service delivery methods and improve interaction between the government and citizens.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the residents of Geneva who will benefit from improved public services, as well as the government officials and departments involved in implementing the AI solutions.

name: AI for Government Training Programme
organisation: Ethiopian Artificial Intelligence Institute, Addis Ababa University, United Nations Development Programme
description: A training programme designed to enhance the artificial intelligence capabilities of Ethiopian federal government employees, focusing on improving service delivery and institutional efficiency through AI.
novelty: The programme introduces government employees to generative AI systems and prompt engineering techniques, emphasizing practical application in public administration.
stakeholders: Federal government employees in Ethiopia, Ethiopian Artificial Intelligence Institute, Addis Ababa University, United Nations Development Programme.

name: AI Agents for Public Service Technology Redevelopment
organisation: Ministry of Technology and Innovation, Government of Alberta
description: The Alberta government is using advanced AI tools developed by Anthropic and Google to overhaul its decades-old public service technology. This initiative aims to modernize critical systems, enhance cybersecurity, and reduce costs significantly. The project includes the development of AI agents that can independently review and update computer code, replacing 165 systems with 16 new applications owned by the government.
novelty: The project is innovative due to its use of AI agents, specifically developed using Anthropic’s Claude AI models, to perform tasks that would traditionally take years in just hours. It demonstrates a practical approach to addressing technical debt and security issues in legacy systems, setting a precedent for other governments.
stakeholders: The primary stakeholders include the Alberta government, public servants, and the general public of Alberta who will benefit from improved public service technology. Other governments can also benefit from the open-source Velocity White Papers shared by Alberta.

name: Claude Code Implementation
description: The Government of Alberta implemented Claude Code with Opus and Sonnet models to review and secure its systems by scanning 466 million lines of code in 20 hours, identifying and fixing vulnerabilities, and modernizing outdated systems.
novelty: The innovation lies in the use of AI to perform large-scale, rapid security reviews and code modernization, achieving in hours what would traditionally take years.
stakeholders: The stakeholders include the Alberta Ministry of Technology and Innovation, government workers, the public, and potentially other governments looking to adopt similar practices.
